questions.question: What is the purpose of CSS?
choices: To define the structure of a web page
To control the visual presentation and styling of HTML elements
To add interactivity to a web page
To store data on the server
questions.explanation: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) controls the visual presentation of HTML elements, including colors, fonts, spacing, and layout.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to link an external CSS file?
questions.explanation: The <link> tag with rel="stylesheet" and href pointing to the CSS file is used to connect an external stylesheet to an HTML document.

questions.question: In the CSS rule `p { color: blue; }`, what is `p`?
questions.explanation:
The `p` is a selector — it targets all

elements on the page. The selector tells the browser which elements to apply the styles to.

questions.question: Which CSS property changes the text color of an element?
questions.explanation: The `color` property sets the text color of an element. Despite the generic name, it applies specifically to text content.

questions.question: The CSS property ___ changes the size of text.
questions.explanation: The font-size property controls the size of text. Values can be in pixels (px), em, rem, percentages, or keywords.

questions.question: The CSS property ___ sets the background color of an element.
questions.explanation: The background-color property sets the background color of an element. It accepts color names, hex codes, RGB, and other color values.
